data.title: South Korean Won Falls on Dollar Strength
data.description:
The South Korean won weakened to around 1,365 per dollar on Friday, extending its downward trend as it tracked broader dollar strength following a stronger-than-expected US nonfarm payrolls report which reduced expectations for Fed rate cuts.

While South Korea posted a solid current account surplus of $10.14 billion in May, fundamentally supportive of the won, it was outweighed by global USD strength.

On the trade front, uncertainty continues to weigh on sentiment ahead of the July 9 US tariff deadline.

Trade Minister Yeo is scheduled to visit Washington this weekend in a last-minute push to secure an extension of the current three-month pause.

The outcome of the talks is critical, as it could impact key exports and add further pressure on the KRW.

data.title: BMG Resources Seeks Trading Halt
data.description:
BMG Resources Ltd :

  • SEEKS TRADING HALT PENDING ANNOUNCEMENT ON MOU FOR POTENTIAL PROCESSING OF ORE FROM CO'S ABERCROMBY GOLD PROJECT

data.title: Sinohydro Bureau International Engineering's Unit Signs 5.06 Billion Yuan Deal for Guinea's Bauxite Mining Project
data.description:
Sinohydro Bureau International Engineering, a unit of Power Construction Corp. of China or PowerChina, signed a 5.06 billion yuan contract for the Guinea Kebo bauxite mining and transportation project in Guinea's Telimele Madina Dian Region, according to a Shanghai bourse disclosure on Friday.

The contract was signed with Guinea KEBO Huaxing Mining and Huaxing Gaoke (Beijing) Business Development.

The main scope of work includes bauxite exploration, overburden stripping, waste excavation, mining, and transportation over a mining area of 204.37 square kilometers. The construction is expected to take 72 months, the Chinese state-owned engineering construction company said.

The mine is expected to produce 28 million tons of bauxite every year.

data.title: Stellantis Recalls Certain Diesel Models in Europe Over Premature Camshaft Wear
data.description:
Stellantis (STLAM.MI, ) announced a recall for certain diesel cars in Europe on Thursday due to potential camshaft chain issues.

The automaker recalled vehicles produced between October 2017 and January 2023. The company did not provide an overall figure but said 930,000 vehicles were affected in France and 117,000 in Belgium.

Impacted models include Peugeot, Citroen, Opel/Vauxhall, DS, and Fiat vehicles that are equipped with the 1.5 BlueHDi diesel engine. The automaker said the vehicles may face premature wear of the camshaft chain, which could cause abnormal noise and breakage in the worst case.

data.title: China Offers Subsidies to Boost Birthrate, Spur Growth
data.description:
China is set to introduce nationwide cash subsidies to boost childbirth amid a persistent population decline that poses long-term economic risks, Bloomberg News reported Friday.

Under the proposed policy, families would receive CNY 3,600 annually for each child born on or after January 1, 2025, continuing until the child turns three.

Though not yet officially announced, the move signals Beijing’s growing urgency to tackle demographic challenges.

Despite ending the one-child policy nearly a decade ago, the country’s birthrate has continued to fall, with only 9.54 million births recorded in 2023—nearly half the 2016 total.

Local governments reportedly have already begun offering incentives, including housing subsidies and lump-sum payments.

data.title: Saudi Enaya Cooperative Insurance Gets Qualification Renewal from Saudi Regulator
data.description:
Saudi Enaya Cooperative Insurance Co. (SASE:8311) secured a qualification renewal from Saudi Arabia's Insurance Authority on Wednesday.

The renewal is valid for one year starting from July 22, 2025, to July 21, 2026, the insurance company said Thursday.

data.title: Market Chatter: AstraZeneca, Summit in Talks for Potential $15 Billion Partnership on Lung-cancer Drug
data.description:
AstraZeneca is negotiating a potential partnership with Summit Therapeutics on the latter's ivonescimab treatment for lung cancer, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The unnamed sources said the biopharmaceutical company could pay as much as $15 billion in total under the deal being considered. The terms are still being finalized, the sources added.

AstraZeneca declined to comment, while Summit did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Lightning Minerals to Start Drilling at Queensland Project; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Lightning Minerals is preparing for drilling at the Mt Turner project in Queensland, one of the assets to be bought under Lotus Minerals' proposed acquisition, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The first phase drill program, which aims to identify gold mineralization beneath existing pits, will begin in eight weeks with site preparations underway, the filing said.

Next week, soil sampling will begin along nine kilometers of the Drummer Hill fault system to examine coherent linear gold anomalies, per the filing.

Shares rose nearly 3% in midday trade Friday.

data.title: Australia's Cleanaway Waste Management rises as Contract Resources deal gets regulator approval
data.description:
** Shares of Cleanaway Waste Management rise as much as 2.2% to A$2.810, their highest level since June 4

** The Australian Competition & Consumer Commission says it does not oppose Cleanaway's acquisition of Contract Resources Group

** Earlier the waste management company had said the acquisition deal will deliver around A$12 million in annual net cost synergies

** The acquisition is expected to be completed by July 31

** Stock up 6% YTD, including the day's move

data.title: Australia's Lottery Corp set for worst day in 5 weeks; Citi flags dour outlook
data.description:
** Lottery Corp slips 2.4% to A$5.25; set for weakest session since May 30

** Stock second weakest performer on benchmark ASX200 index , which is nearly flat

** Citi downgrades to "sell" from "buy", cuts PT to A$5 from A$5.60

** Expects FY25 EBIT of A$628 mln ($412 mln), down ~13% y/y

** Says like-for-like sales of co's game brand Powerball worsened in June; expects weakness to carry through to H1 FY26

** Tempers its outlook for increased Powerball turnover following game changes planned for late FY26

** TLC gains 1.7% so far this week, set for strongest weekly performance since mid-June

($1 = A$1.5235)

data.title: Starlight Retail Joins Google Cloud Partner Advantage Program to Deliver Advanced Cloud and AI Solutions in Pakistan
data.description:
LAHORE, Pakistan, July 03, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Starlight Retail, a global technology solutions provider, announced today that it has joined the Google Cloud Partner Advantage program as a Partner level partner for the Service engagement model. This collaboration gives Google Cloud customers in Pakistan and across the region access to Starlight Retail’s comprehensive suite of technology services and solutions, designed to accelerate digital transformation and drive business growth.

As businesses in Pakistan increasingly look to modernize their operations, the demand for scalable, secure, and intelligent cloud solutions has grown significantly. This collaboration positions Starlight Retail to meet this demand by combining its deep expertise in custom application development and managed services with the robust, global infrastructure of Google Cloud.

As a Google Cloud partner, Starlight Retail will offer customers specialized services that leverage the scale, security, and innovation of Google Cloud’s infrastructure. This includes custom SaaS application development on Google Cloud, deployment of advanced cybersecurity measures using Google Cloud’s security portfolio, and the creation of intelligent business solutions with Google Cloud’s AI and machine learning capabilities. By working with Starlight Retail, organizations can unlock new efficiencies and capabilities, enabling them to compete more effectively in a digital-first economy.

Key features of the offering include:

  • Custom SaaS and Application Development: Building and deploying scalable, secure, and high-performance applications on Google Cloud to meet unique business requirements.
  • Advanced Cybersecurity Services: Implementing robust security postures for cloud environments, leveraging Google Cloud’s native security tools to protect critical data and infrastructure.
  • AI and Data Analytics Implementation: Helping clients unlock insights from their data and build intelligent applications using Google Cloud’s BigQuery, Looker, and Vertex AI platforms.
  • Managed Cloud Services: Providing end-to-end management of Google Cloud environments to optimize cost, performance, and reliability, allowing clients to focus on their core business.

“Joining the Google Cloud Partner Advantage program is a pivotal step in our mission to empower businesses through technology,” said Mammon Baloch, CEO of Starlight Retail. “Our presence in Pakistan is focused on helping local enterprises compete on a global scale. This collaboration allows us to combine our expertise in tailored solution delivery with the world-class infrastructure of Google Cloud so that we can provide our clients with the tools they need to innovate faster, operate more securely, and unlock new opportunities for growth.”

To learn more about how Starlight Retail can help your business leverage Google Cloud, visit or schedule a consultation today.

Starlight Retail Inc., established in 2021, is a Los Angeles, California-based global technology company. With a presence in eight countries, including Pakistan, Starlight Retail specializes in Software as a Service (SaaS), tech automation, and advanced digital solutions. The company is dedicated to transforming the tech industry through a focus on innovation, excellence, and customer satisfaction. Starlight Retail partners with prominent technology providers to offer a diverse range of solutions that enable businesses to improve efficiency, strengthen security, and foster growth in the digital age.

Media Contact:

Shehroze Abbas Shah

+92 304 1400273

shehroze@starlightretailinc.com

data.title: HDFC Bank's June-quarter average deposits rise 5.1% sequentially
data.description:
HDFC Bank's June-quarter average deposits rose 5.1% sequentially, India's top private lender said on Friday.

The bank's gross advances at the end of June were 0.4% higher from last quarter, it said.

data.title: Dy6 Metals Seeks Trading Halt
data.description:
DY6 Metals Ltd :

  • SEEKS TRADING HALT PENDING ANNOUNCEMENT ON PROPOSED ACQUISITION

data.title: Philippines' Headline Consumer Inflation Edges Up to 1.4% in June
data.description:
The Philippines' headline inflation edged up to 1.4% in June from 1.3% in the previous month, according to data from the Philippine Statistics Authority on Friday.

The quicker inflation was mainly driven by a higher annual increment in the index of housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels at 3.2% during the month from 2.3% in the previous month.

Food inflation eased to 0.1% in June from 0.7% in May, the PSA said.

Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, remained at 2.2% in June for the fourth month running, the data showed.

data.title: Fantasia Again Extends Deadline of Debt Restructuring Agreement; Shares Fall 4%
data.description:
Fantasia Holdings Group again extended the long-stop date of its restructuring support agreement amid ongoing discussions with stakeholders, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the property developer fell over 4% in morning trade Monday.

The long stop date was extended to July 11 from July 3 previously.

The firm entered into an agreement for its proposed offshore debt restructuring in April 2024.

data.title: Ta Yang Group to Raise HK$34.4 Million via Share Subscription; Shares Soar 29%
data.description:
Ta Yang Group Holdings plans to issue 28.66 million shares at HK$1.20 each to subscribers under a general mandate, according to a late Thursday filing with the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

Shares of the company surged 29% in Friday morning trade.

The subscription price represents a premium of about 90.48% to the stock's last closing price of HK$0.63 and an 83.49% premium to the five-day average. The new shares will represent 16.66% of the company's enlarged share capital.

Net proceeds are expected to be around HK$34.39 million and will be used as general working capital, including support for the group's digital marketing business.

data.title: New Zealand's Vital Ltd hits over 3-year high as board recommends Tait's buyout offer
data.description:
** Shares of Vital Ltd rise 3.6% to NZ$0.435, their highest level since Feb 25, 2022

** On track for biggest pct gain since June 19, if gains hold

** Telecom services co's board recommends accepting private firm Tait International's takeover offer of NZ$0.45 per VTL share, a 7% premium on stock's last close

** YTD, stock up 70.6%, including moves in current session

data.title: Palm falls on weaker rivals, but poised for weekly gain
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures opened lower on Friday, weighed down by weaker rival edible oils, but were on track for a seventh weekly gain in eight.

The benchmark palm oil contract for September delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange slid 21 ringgit, or 0.51%, to 4,070 ringgit ($962.17) a metric ton in early trade. The contract has risen 1.67% so far this week.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) fell 0.82%, while its palm oil contract shed 0.12%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade were down 0.96%.

* Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils, as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

* Oil prices were little changed as a solid U.S. job market bolstered the case for the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates on hold, with investors also awaiting clarity on President Donald Trump's plans for tariffs on various countries. O/R

* Weaker crude oil futures make palm a less attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

* The ringgit , palm's currency of trade, weakened 0.24% against the dollar, making the commodity cheaper for buyers holding foreign currencies.

* Malaysia's palm oil inventories likely dropped for the first time in four months in June as production fell unexpectedly while export demand remained robust for the tropical oil, a Reuters survey showed.

* Palm oil may fall to 4,047 ringgit per ton, as a five-wave cycle from 3,947 ringgit has completed, Reuters technical analyst Wang Tao said. TECH/C

MARKET NEWS

* Most Asian equity markets struggled, despite record highs for Wall Street overnight, as Trump's deadline for trade deals loomed next week. MKTS/GLOB

DATA/EVENTS

0600 Germany Industrial Orders MM, Manufacturing O/P Cur Price SA, Consumer Goods SA May

0830 UK S&P GLOBAL PMI: MSC COMPOSITE - OUTPUT June

($1 = 4.2300 ringgit)

data.title: Steadfast Looks Cheap Compared With Global Brokers — Market Talk
data.description: Steadfast's bulls at Macquarie continue to see the Australian insurance broker as attractively priced relative to its global peers. In the long-term, the investment bank's analysts see Steadfast's ability to maximize returns on a U.S. rollout as key to the stock's value. More immediately, they tell clients in a note that shares are trading at a near 21% discount to international brokers. This compares with its 2.3% premium over the long term, they add. Supporting their positive view, they calculate that a portfolio with Steadfast's product mix would have achieved 7.2% pricing growth in the June quarter. Macquarie keeps an outperform rating and A$6.80 target price on the stock, which is up 1.1% at A$6.015. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0319 GMT - Steadfast's bulls at Macquarie continue to see the Australian insurance broker as attractively priced relative to its global peers. In the long-term, the investment bank's analysts see Steadfast's ability to maximize returns on a U.S. rollout as key to the stock's value. More immediately, they tell clients in a note that shares are trading at a near 21% discount to international brokers. This compares with its 2.3% premium over the long term, they add. Supporting their positive view, they calculate that a portfolio with Steadfast's product mix would have achieved 7.2% pricing growth in the June quarter. Macquarie keeps an outperform rating and A$6.80 target price on the stock, which is up 1.1% at A$6.015. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0258 GMT - Pro Medicus's bulls at Morgan Stanley reckon the imaging-tech provider's contracts look more like evergreen licenses than fixed-period agreements. Highlighting the Australia-listed company's near zero levels of historical churn, MS analysts estimate that its two new contracts have a lifetime value of more than A$5 billion. This is equivalent to 20% of the company's current equity market capitalization, they observe. They tell clients in a note that this estimate could fall if a new competitor enters the market, although it could also rise with price increases or new products. MS keeps an overweight rating on the stock and lifts its target price by 3.2% to A$320.00. Shares are down 0.3% at A$306.42. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0242 GMT - The Singapore retail staples sector's earnings outlook seems more positive, RHB Research's Alfie Yeo says in a research report. RHB sees Sheng Siong Group's growth riding on more new outlets amid a robust supermarket retail sales environment in Singapore. Also, DFI Retail Group's initiative to boost profitability by divesting less profitable non-core businesses is helping earnings to recover, the analyst says. Positive 1Q results of both companies prompt RHB to raise 2025-2027 earnings estimates for DFI Retail Group by 2% and for Sheng Siong by 4% for 2025 and 6% each for 2026 and 2027. RHB has buy ratings on both stocks and a target price of S$2.12 on Sheng Siong and US$3.09 on DFI Retail Group. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0225 GMT - Malaysia's glove sector may see a rebound in 2H albeit at a slower pace, as low inventories prompt order replenishment and global buyers shift from Chinese producers due to rising tariffs, says Kenanga IB analyst Raymond Choo Ping Khoon in a note. Potential policy changes in the EU and the U.S. on Chinese gloves could drive up demand, he says. Despite near-term earnings weakness, the sector's long-term prospects may remain positive, supported by structural demand growth and ongoing supply rationalization, he adds. Kenanga maintains an overweight call on the sector, favoring Hartalega and Kossan Rubber Industries for exposure. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0215 GMT - Mr. D.I.Y. Group (M) refreshing its MR.TOY brand with a more experiential store format is aimed at boosting engagement and basket sizes, Kenanga IB analyst Thin Yun Jing says in a note. MR.TOY is the Malaysian home-improvement retailer's toy store chain. While earnings impact remains limited for now, the new concept could serve as a blueprint to scale and widen appeal, she says. Kenanga raises the stock's target price to MYR2.04 from MYR1.80 as its rolls forward valuation base year, and maintains an outperform rating on the stock. Shares are unchanged at MYR1.66. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0213 GMT - Tencent Music Entertainment's acquisition of podcast platform Ximalaya fits well with its latest subscription initiative, Citi analysts say in a research note. TME has been working on converting subscribers to premium subscribers, which proves to be a more sustainable and resilient approach to ensure steady music subscription revenue growth. The addition of Ximalaya's long-form content to TME platform could enhance monetization potential through subscriber conversion and ads. While maintaining a buy rating on TME, Citi raises its target price to US$23.00 from US$17.00, noting the stable subscription model with improving margins profile and future upside of artists/fan-based entertainment ecosystem fits well with the growing service consumption among Chinese young consumers. Shares last ended at US$19.46. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0205 GMT - Bangkok Chain Hospital's EPS growth outlook of 17% for 2025 and 10% for 2026 is likely the strongest in the sector, Thanachart Securities' Siriporn Arunothai says in a research report. This outlook is backed by rising revenue from both Thai and international cash patients, increased income from a social security scheme and improving EBIT margins. Also, the hospital is likely seeing minor impact from the Thailand-Cambodia border dispute, while its stock is trading at an attractive 1.4x 2025 price/earnings-to-growth ratio compared with the sector average of 5.0x, the analyst adds. The brokerage has a buy rating and a target price of THB19.00 on the shares, which last closed at THB13.30. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0158 GMT - The Malaysian consumer sector's 3Q earnings are likely to be weighed by softer spending due to rising living costs and domestic policy uncertainties, says Affin Hwang IB. Consumer spending may slow amid the expansion of service tax and the impending RON95 fuel subsidy changes, says analyst Peggie Wong Pei Chi in a note. However, sentiment could rebound in 4Q, supported by festive demand and improved policy clarity. Affin Hwang maintains a neutral outlook on Malaysia's consumer sector and prefers AEON Co. (M) and Heineken Malaysia for their resilience. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0144 GMT - South Korean auto stocks appear to be trading at a discount with their fundamentals and opportunities in physical artificial intelligence seemingly overlooked, Daiwa Capital analysts Henny Jung and Yoonki Bae say in a note. The new Seoul government's pledge to invest 100 trillion won, equivalent to $73.34 billion, in AI and expand related infrastructure will likely accelerate the commercialization of autonomous driving and humanoid robots, where Hyundai Motor Group is already active through its affiliates, the analysts say. They choose the group's flagship carmaker Hyundai Motor and its auto-component-making affiliate Hyundai Mobis as their top picks in the local auto sector. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0137 GMT - Malaysia's oil-and-gas sector is expected to see steady upstream activity and strong midstream gas demand, MIDF says in a note. Liquefied natural gas infrastructure growth, rising power needs from data centers, and regional energy transition efforts should support midstream resilience, it says. However, weak petrochemical margins and potential oil oversupply remain key risks, it adds. While geopolitical tensions may lift short-term prices, MIDF maintains a neutral rating on the sector. It favors MISC for its diversified earnings and exposure to long-haul shipping and floating production storage and offloading vessel projects.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0120 GMT - Pro Medicus looks too pricey for Morgans analyst Iain Wilkie to chase hard after the stock, even with the medical imaging-tech provider's strong start to the fiscal year. Wilkie maintains a trim recommendation on the Australia-listed stock. Pro Medicus's two new contracts are viewed positively, but Wilkie says in a note that it is hard to see how the company can increase its growth rate of recent years. The number of large contract opportunities in the pipeline is diminishing, he adds. Morgans raises its target price 12% to A$280.00. Shares are up 0.1% at A$307.58. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0118 GMT - Malaysian banks may perform well ahead of an anticipated market recovery in late 2H, Hong Leong IB analyst Eddy Do says in a note. He sees 2H as a period of stark contrast for the domestic equities market, with 3Q expected to remain volatile before conditions stabilize in 4Q. However, the market appears increasingly desensitized to trade-related rhetoric, suggesting peak uncertainty may have passed. The banking sector's defensive earnings resilience, attractive dividend yields, and undemanding valuations could provide downside support amid the market volatility, he adds. Hong Leong maintains an overweight rating on Malaysia's banking sector, and pegs CIMB Group, AMMB Holdings and RHB Bank as top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Poly Developments Buys Real Estate Projects for 8.61 Billion Yuan
data.description:
Poly Developments and Holdings acquired five real estate projects for 8.61 billion yuan, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

The projects are located in the Chinese cities of Shanghai, Shijiazhuang, Qingdao, and Sanya. The projects have a total land area of 164,884 square meters and a planned gross floor area of 320,327 square meters, the Chinese diversified property developer said.

data.title: Australian Mines Signs Term Sheet to Earn-In to Brazil Project; Shares Down 9%
data.description:
Australian Mines signed a term sheet with Cabral Resources, a subsidiary of Toronto Stock Exchange-listed GoldMining and Majestic D&M, to earn-in to the Boa Vista project in Brazil, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company may earn up to an 80% interest in the project through staged exploration and development expenditure and milestone-based achievements, with an initial consideration of CA$55,000 for a 90-day exclusivity period, the filing said.

Australian Mines shares fell 9% in midday trade Friday.

data.title: Indian shares to open flat after regulator bars Jane Street from local market
data.description:
India's benchmark equity indexes are expected to open largely flat on Friday as investors assess the market regulator's move to bar U.S. trading company Jane Street from the local securities market over alleged manipulation.

The Gift Nifty futures (GIFc1) was down 0.2% at 25,516 points, as of 8:14 a.m. IST, indicating that the Nifty 50 will hover around its previous close of 25,405.3.

The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has passed an interim order on Jane Street's alleged manipulation through positions taken in equities derivatives.

The regulator said in its order that Jane Street would no longer be able to participate in India's stock market, and that it would "impound" the trading company's 48.4 billion rupees ($566.71 million), which it said were the "unlawful gains earned" from the alleged misconduct.

SEBI's actions come as half a dozen global trading firms, including Citadel Securities, IMC Trading, Millennium and Optiver, are ratcheting up their presence in India's booming derivatives markets.

"While it may stir some immediate nervousness, it appears case-specific and is unlikely to prompt broader FPI unwinding," said Kranthi Bathini, director - equity strategy at WealthMills securities.

Meanwhile, investors are awaiting a potential trade deal between India and the U.S. ahead of President Donald Trump's July 9 deadline for punitive reciprocal tariffs.

Other Asian markets fell on the day, while Wall Street equities and the dollar rallied after stronger-than-expected U.S jobs data indicated resilience in the labour market.

STOCKS TO WATCH

** Bajaj Finance posts a 15% rise in deposit book in June quarter to 721 billion rupees; assets under management (AUM) rises 25%

** PC Jeweller achieves 80% revenue growth in June quarter and trims outstanding debts by 7.5%

** Marico says it posted a low-twenties percentage growth in consolidated revenue in the June quarter, aided by rural demand

** Bank of Baroda posts a 9.1% year-on-year growth in global deposits in the first quarter of fiscal 2026

data.title: Market Chatter: Celltrion Holdings to Buy KRW500 Billion in Celltrion Shares, Eyes Portfolio Expansion
data.description:
Celltrion Holdings, the holding company of Celltrion Inc. , will purchase 500 billion won worth of the latter's shares from August to boost its core affiliate's value and enhance shareholder returns, Yonhap News reported Friday.

The move is part of a broader 1 trillion won investment plan aimed at improving profitability and transitioning into an operating holding firm. The company also plans to acquire a high-value domestic or overseas business, the report said.

Celltrion shares are down 8.1% this year amid US tariffs and drug pricing concerns, it said.

Shares of Celltrion Inc. rose nearly 3% in recent trade.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Zimi to Raise AU$1.4 Million Via Share Placement
data.description:
Zimi received firm commitments to raise around AU$1.4 million through the issue of 240.5 million shares to institutional and sophisticated investors at AU$0.006 each, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The issue price represents a 25% discount to the company's last trading price, the filing said.

Proceeds will be used to fund manufacturing costs, new product research and development, the filing added.

Cerberus Investments acted as lead manager for the placement.

data.title: Singapore Dollar Consolidates Amid Concerns Over U.S. Tariffs — Market Talk
data.description: The Singapore dollar consolidates against its U.S. counterpart during the Asian session amid concerns over tariffs. If U.S. President Trump unilaterally reimposes high tariffs on July 9 and/or on August 12, when his two tariff truces expire, foreign-exchange markets could become highly volatile, says CBA's Joseph Capurso in a research report. A "large currency reaction is possible because we consider market participants are complacent as illustrated by low implied volatility," says the head of International and Sustainable Economics. USD/SGD is little changed at 1.2746. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 11 PM ET: U.S. Stocks Reach Another Closing High After Jobs Report Stronger Than Expected | Wall ...
data.description:
U.S. Stocks Reach Another Closing High After Jobs Report Stronger Than Expected

The S&P 500 rose to a record for the seventh time this year, while the Nasdaq also ended the shortened week at an all-time high.

----

Wall Street Worries as Crisis-Level Deficits Become the Government's Default Mode

New legislation could push federal debt into dangerous territory-without an emergency requiring it.

----

House Passes Trump's Megabill in GOP Triumph

The narrow vote delivered the president a major second-term legislative victory.

----

Canada Goods Trade Deficit Narrows as Flows With U.S. Continue to Slacken

Helped by volatile gold shipments, the May deficit was down from a record in April and continued to reflect fluctuating tariff policy in Canada's biggest trade partner and neighbor.

----

Trump Says He Made No Progress With Putin on Ukraine in Call

He urged Russia's leader to quickly end the war in Ukraine, according to a Kremlin briefing on the hourlong call.

----

Want to Know Where the Economy Is Heading? It's All About Earnings.

June jobs data handily beat expectations. But the report was full of asterisks. What matters is tariffs, inflation, and corporate profits.

----

The Dow and Russell 2000 are joining the stock market's party. Is it a game changer for the bulls?

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite tallied fresh record closing highs on Thursday, buoyed by a stronger-than-expected jobs report that helped dampen expectations for a Federal Reserve interest-rate cut in July.

----

June Jobs Report Likely to Extend Fed's Summer Pause

Fed officials have cited solid labor-market readings to justify a wait-and-see stance.

----

Summer Road Trips to Drive Record Ethanol Use

A record number of drivers are hitting the road this summer, giving the U.S. ethanol industry a needed shot in the arm.

----

Steady Hiring Added 147,000 Jobs to U.S. Economy in June

The jobs report showed manufacturers are pausing hiring amid tariff uncertainty, while unemployment fell partly because fewer people are looking for work.

----

Biggest part of the U.S. economy rebounds from trade-war blues. But it's still weak.

The large service side of the economy rebounded in June after a brief slump in the prior month when U.S. trade wars were at their zenith and companies were paralyzed by indecision.

----

Tesla Stock Diehards Don't Give an Inch

Challenges are piling up for the electric-vehicle maker and CEO Elon Musk, but the company's legion of individual investors are staying put.

----

Vietnam Trade Deal Takes Aim at Back Door for Chinese Goods

The deal with Vietnam seeks to stop Chinese-made goods from transiting through the Southeast Asian country to avoid higher duties.

----

A Few Members Saw a Case for Unchanged Key Rate in June, ECB Says

The fact they already considered keeping rates on hold will likely underpin expectations that the central bank will leave its key rate unchanged later this month.

----

U.K. Government Bonds, Pound Recover After Prime Minister Backs Treasury Chief

Keir Starmer initially refused to confirm if Rachel Reeves would remain in post, causing sterling and gilts to sell off sharply.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 11 PM ET: Hilco Global to Sell Majority Stake, Launch Direct Lending Unit | Meta ...
data.description:
Hilco Global to Sell Majority Stake, Launch Direct Lending Unit

The U.S. arm of Japanese investment firm Orix has agreed to acquire 70% of Hilco and commit $1 billion to its new lending business focusing on private credit, sources say.

----

Meta Offers to Buy Stake in Venture Funds Started by AI Hires Nat Friedman and Daniel Gross

Limited partners could cash out of a minority of their holdings in venture firm NFDG at current values in a secondary deal with Meta.

----

UPS Offers Buyouts to Drivers, a First in Its 117-Year History

The company is cutting costs because of flat parcel volumes, rising labor costs and a long stock-price slide.

----

Apollo-Backed Athora Buys U.K. Insurer for $7.8 Billion

The acquisition of Pension Insurance Corp. would effectively turn the specialist insurer into Athora's U.K. insurance business.

----

The Corporate Winners and Losers in Trump's Big Tax Bill

Oil drillers win access to public lands and manufacturers get tax breaks, while elite universities face new endowment taxes and solar projects lose tax breaks.

----

These chip-design software stocks are climbing after a U.S.-China trade deal

Siemens, Synopsys and Cadence said the U.S. has lifted export controls restricting sales and business in China.

----

Thoma Bravo to Take Olo Private in $2 Billion All-Cash Deal

Olo shareholders will receive $10.25 a share, a 65% premium over the share price prior to deal speculation. Shares jumped on the announcement.

----

Krispy Kreme Finance Chief Jeremiah Ashukian Leaving

Krispy Kreme's top finance executive, Jeremiah Ashukian, is leaving the doughnut maker to pursue an opportunity with a private company.

----

Burberry Got Too Fancy. This American Is Taking It Back to Its British Roots.

Scarves, umbrellas and the classic trench are key to CEO Joshua Schulman's revival of the luxury brand.

----

The Score: Nike, Paramount, Constellation and More Stocks That Defined the Week

Here are some of the major companies whose stocks moved on the week's news.

----

Airbus, ASML, Mistral Ask EU to Pause AI Rules

CEOs said the bloc's ability to become a leader in nascent AI is being disrupted by overlapping and complicated regulations.

----

JPMorgan Is Revamping Its Bank for the Superrich to Cater to Global Clientele

David Frame is named global head of the private bank, helping deep-pocketed clients invest more abroad.

----

Gold Reserve Emerges as Top Bidder for Venezuela's Citgo

A court-appointed special master recommended a bid backed by Gold Reserve and Koch over a rival hedge-fund offer.

----

Tesla Is in Disarray. Musk Has Already Moved Beyond Caring About Cars.

The electric-vehicle maker has an aging lineup and slumping sales, but its CEO has shifted his focus to robotaxis and robots.

data.title: Taaleem's Fiscal Q3 Profit Falls; Revenue Up
data.description:
Taaleem Holdings (DFM:TAALEEM) said Thursday its profit for the fiscal third quarter dropped, while revenue rose year over year.

Profit for the three months ended May 31 was 82 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 86.4 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.08 dirham from 0.09 dirham.

The Dubai-listed education company's revenue was 335.4 million dirhams, compared with 282.1 million dirhams earlier.
